A DesignTimeDbContextFactory using Npgsql with snake_case mapping is needed.

_**Agent (default)**_

The migration was generated for SQLite while runtime uses PostgreSQL with snake_case — I'll add a design-time factory and regenerate the migration for Npgsql.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The application failed because the `SCHAERBEEK_PASSWORD` environment variable contains characters that EF Core cannot substitute. This caused the database migration command to fail. The password value needs to be corrected.

_**Agent (default)**_

The startup crash came from a **provider mismatch** between how the migration was generated and how the app runs under Aspire.

## Root cause

The `InitialCreate` migration was generated for **SQLite** (`TEXT` columns, SQLite-style primary keys). At runtime, Aspire wires up **PostgreSQL** with snake_case naming:

```28:29:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/DependencyInjection.cs
                options.UseNpgsql(connectionString)
                    .UseSnakeCaseNamingConvention();
```

EF Core 10 treats that mismatch as pending model changes and throws during `MigrateAsync()` in Development — which matches your stack trace.

This aligns with [ADR-0002](docs/adr/0002-test-database-strategy.md): migrations must target Npgsql, while tests use SQLite with `EnsureCreated()`.

## What I fixed

1. Added `MunicipalDbContextFactory` so `dotnet ef` always generates **PostgreSQL** migrations (Npgsql + snake_case), regardless of the default SQLite fallback in `DependencyInjection`.

2. Regenerated `InitialCreate` with proper PostgreSQL types (`uuid`, `character varying(64)`).

Verified: `dotnet ef migrations has-pending-model-changes` reports no drift, and integration tests pass.

## Next step

Restart the AppHost:

```bash
dotnet run --project src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.AppHost
```

If it still fails on an old database volume, reset the Aspire PostgreSQL container (remove its Docker volume) so migrations apply to a clean database.

---

The `Substitution is not possible for environment variable ConnectionStrings__schaerbeek` lines are Aspire reconciliation noise — the connection string is still passed through correctly. They are unrelated to the migration error.
